calendar.matters: ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Aug-01-2019 Continued To Nov-05-19 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 514

Notice Of Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Mataese Togafau
Off Calendar Jul-15-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT EXPERT SERVICE PROFESSIONALS, INC.'s Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment.

Defendant Expert Service Professionals, Inc.'s ("ESP") motion to vacate default and default judgment is denied.

First, the motion is untimely. Code of Civil Procedure 473.5 provides that the motion must be "filed within a reasonable time, but in no event exceeding the earlier of: (i) two years after entry of a default judgment against him or her; or (ii) 180 days after service on him or her of a written notice that the default or default judgment has been entered." In this case, the notice of entry of judgment was served on August 23, 2018, and plaintiff filed this untimely motion on June 19, 2019. Even if the court were to rely on the 2-year deadline, the court concludes that the motion is still untimely because ESP failed to move within "a reasonable time." The record demonstrates that ESP's Chief Financial Officer (Paul Alvarez) was served with an order of examination in January of 2019, but ESP failed to file this motion earlier. (Kirkgaard Decl. 28 and Ex. S.) Moreover, the record shows that Mr. Alvarez appeared in this action to contest service of an order of examination in March of 2018. (Id.) While it may be true that Mr. Alvarez was appearing in his individual capacity or on behalf of Telic Insurance Services at that time, constructive notice is still imputed to ESP as he was ESP's agent.

Second, ESP fails to provide a proposed pleading in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 473.5(b).

Third, even in light of the liberal policy to hear cases on the merits, ESP fails to carry its evidentiary burden of showing a lack of actual notice. The record discloses that numerous documents regarding this case had been properly served at ESP's office over the years. (Summons and complaint [8/29/16]; request for entry of default [11/4/16]; application for default judgment [10/24/17]; notice of entry of order granting default judgment [12/20/17].) The terse declaration of ESP's president (Mataese Togagau) does not sufficiently explain why or how these documents did not reach it. The court recognizes that mail may get lost or misplaced. ESP, however, concedes that the documents were mailed to the correct address, and the sheer number of documents that ESP allegedly did not receive makes its position implausible.

A proposed order is submitted by email. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPU)
